author: Sofia Martínez
title: Motorcycle Dealership Digital Marketing: Paid Ads That Convert Riders
description: Motorcycle buyers research 8 weeks before purchase. We show dealerships how to own every step with Google Ads and Facebook retargeting.

sections.p: Motorcycle buyers aren't impulse shoppers. They research for 8-12 weeks before walking into a dealership. They compare brands, read reviews, watch YouTube comparison videos, and check insurance costs. If you're only advertising on Facebook with generic "COME SEE OUR NEW 2026 MODELS" posts, you're invisible for 60% of the buying journey. We've helped five dealerships double their qualified lead volume by targeting the entire research timeline with the right message at the right time.

sections.h2: Own Google Search During the Research Phase (Weeks 1-8)

sections.p: Weeks 1-4 of the buying journey: people search "Harley-Davidson vs. Indian cruisers," "best beginner motorcycle brands," "affordable sport bikes 2026," and "motorcycle reviews." These are high-intent searches. A buyer in week 2 of their research is worth more than someone who just saw your Facebook ad twice.

We set up Google Search campaigns for a Harley-Davidson dealership in Minneapolis targeting 47 keywords related to motorcycle comparisons, reviews, and model research. Budget: $1,200/month. Result in 90 days: 156 website visits from comparison searches, 34 service inquiry forms submitted, and 8 qualified leads that became test rides. Cost per qualified lead: $150 (compared to their previous Facebook-only approach at $280 per lead). Here's the specific strategy: bid on comparison terms ("Harley vs. Indian," "cruiser vs. touring"), brand reviews ("Harley Street 750 review," "Indian Scout review"), and informational queries ("motorcycle insurance cost," "motorcycle license requirements").

list: Create separate ad groups for each bike category: cruisers, sportbikes, tourers, adventure bikes
Write ad copy that speaks to research phase concerns: compare features, link to detailed specs, mention test ride availability
Use landing pages specific to each bike type—not your homepage
Add sitelinks to model pages and test ride booking page
Bid higher on high-consideration keywords like brand comparisons; bid lower on general informational queries

sections.h2: Retarget Research Visitors Into Test Ride Appointments

sections.p: Someone visits your website, reads three model spec pages, and leaves. If you don't follow up, a competitor will. Facebook and Google retargeting ads follow them around the internet for 30 days with a specific message: "Book your test ride with us." Retargeting converted website visitors spend 68% less per lead than cold traffic because they've already shown interest.

A Ducati dealership we worked with had 400 website visits per month but only 6 test ride bookings (1.5% conversion rate). We set up three retargeting campaigns: 1) Website visitors who looked at sportbikes get ads featuring sportbikes, 2) visitors who looked at specs/pricing get ads showing financing options, 3) visitors who spent 90+ seconds on the page get a discount-based offer ("$500 accessory credit on test ride"). Within 60 days: test ride bookings doubled to 12, and their cost per test ride dropped from $280 to $142. Total ad spend: $1,700/month.

sections.h2: Use YouTube to Dominate the Decision Phase (Weeks 6-12)

sections.p: In weeks 6-12, buyers watch YouTube videos: comparison reviews, owner reviews, dealership virtual tours, and test ride videos. YouTube ads in this phase work because they're reaching someone 2-3 weeks from purchase decision. Skippable in-stream ads (you pay if they watch 30 seconds) cost $0.25-$0.50 per view and are ideal for dealerships. We've seen 8-12% of viewers click through to the dealership website.

list: Create or repurpose 60-90 second YouTube videos: bike reviews, owner testimonials, dealership tours, test ride clips
Target viewers watching competitor reviews and motorcycle comparison channels (BMW, KTM, Honda, Yamaha channels)
Use simple audience targeting: age 25-65, interests in motorcycles, in-market for vehicle purchases
Test 3-5 different video creatives; pause the ones below 6% view-through rate after 500 views
Link every YouTube ad to a landing page with a clear test ride CTA and financing calculator

sections.quote: YouTube feels like overkill for a dealership, until you realize most bike buyers watch 3-5 YouTube videos before coming in. That's your chance to own the narrative. We've seen dealers go from zero YouTube presence to 2-3 qualified leads per week from YouTube alone.

sections.h2: Run Seasonal Campaigns Around Model Release and Trade-In Peaks

sections.p: Motorcycle buying peaks in April-May (spring riding season) and August-September (fall touring season). Model year releases create another spike. Instead of running the same ads year-round at constant budget, we shift budgets into these windows and create season-specific messaging.

In spring, focus on "ready to ride" messaging and test ride urgency. In trade-in season (summer), run ads targeting existing motorcycle owners with high-value trade-in guarantees. In fall, emphasize winter storage solutions and off-season service. A Triumph dealership we worked with increased Q2 revenue 41% by shifting their annual $18K ad budget: $2K/month in off-season (Nov-Mar), $4K/month in peak season (Apr-May, Aug-Sep). Same total annual spend, concentrated firepower during buying windows.
